rationale: Fails Step 0: it does not recur (one-time asset) and 'really compelling' is a taste judgment with no tool gate (spin on taste). Recommend a strong one-shot draft, or Template 2 with the human as the real gate.

All five qualifiers pass; bad output is auto-rejectable via tsc --noEmit, a rung-1 computational gate on the actual deliverable (not mere delivery plumbing); done is objective (the compiler exits 0); cheap nightly pass.

A time trigger is not by itself a reason to build an agent loop; delivery confirmation (email sent) is a delivery guard, not a verifier, and the prose summary is subjective and ungated. This is a scheduled draft-generator (cron/Task Scheduler), not an autonomous loop.
Recurs nightly; auto-rejectable via the test suite (a rung-1 tool gate, not the maker self-grading); done is objective (runner exits 0); three stop conditions including a budget cap.

'Make it cleaner' is subjective with no rung-1 gate, so the only verifier left is self-grading (off the ladder); unattended + metered + unproven fails economics; comprehension-debt risk. Use a human-in-the-loop review of each refactor instead.

A tool can confirm a label/comment was posted, not that it is correct or appropriate; posting a public first response is outward-facing and irreversible. The highest viable gate is rung-3 human -> draft-and-queue for approval, so it is human-in-the-loop, not autonomous.

'Best' is irreducibly subjective taste with no tool gate, and auto-publishing exposes off-brand content. Pre-rank candidates on a schedule, then route to a human who approves one before the publish step fires.

Self-grading with the same model is off the verifier ladder (a validator error); unattended on metered billing with no proven economics risks five-figure bills. Needs a rung-1 gate or an independent model checker given the artifact but not the maker's justification, and a proven manual pass first.
